mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment surge, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE growth exceeds 1.0pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST diverge by more than 2 standard deviations, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of co-expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect accelerating manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following positive INDPRO revisions, or the signal reverses direction during periods of capacity utilization above 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 2.5 percent annually triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold, or the negative correlation vanishes when real yields remain below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in >50% of occurrences, or the predictive relationship breaks down when PAYEMS growth falls below 100k while UNRATE simultaneously declines.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real gross domestic product growth above trend levels indicates expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which directly translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following above-trend GDPC1 releases, or the signal shows zero predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 across 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing demand, causing rapid equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in >60% of instances following the specified industrial production drop, or the negative signal is consistently overridden by concurrent monetary easing announcements.
